Note: 43-year-old subject 00015 was a participant in a Merck clinical trial and received 3/4 planned doses of a HIV-1 DNA vaccine containing clade B CAM-1 isolate gag. He was seronegative at enrollment (˜01/2002) but became infected with HIV-1 clade B during the trial (˜04/2002). First detectable VL was 8,300 genomes/ml (04/30/2002), followed by waning levels (5,700 & 1,300 genomes/ml respectively on 5/29/2002 & 7/9/2002), before a stable set point of <1,000 genomes/ml was established. Transmission partner, and likely recipient, of subject 00016. VL, CD4 total count & Gag-specific CTL dynamics shown in Fig. 1 [Balamurugan2008, PMID: 18199650].

44-year-old subject 00016 was a transmission partner, and likely donor, of subject 00015. He was subsequently enrolled in an acute infection cohort run by E.S. Daar in Los Angeles. 00016 had tested negative on 11/15/2001 & 4/10/2002 (the latter when he presented with symptoms) but tested positive on 8/20/2002. VL & CD4 count dynamics shown in Fig. 1 [Balamurugan2008, PMID: 18199650].

Aysmptomatic seropositive subject, 010-035i, also known as Subject 63 had a CD4+ cell count of 145/mm<sup>3</sup> and a clonal cell line, B8-restricted 35D45, was generated from the patient. [Johnson1992, PMID: 137250]. This patient's CTL were capable of lysing autologous Gag-expressing targets and remained stable without significant declines in CD4+ T cells [Johnson1991, PMID: 1715361]. Longitudinal studies were conducted in 035i, who had been infected at least since 1989 and started HAART in 1996. Not much sequence evolution was seen in this patient [Koibuchi2005a, PMID: 15956562].  LANL database note - The HIV Sequence database lists the HLA for this patient, 35I, as A*01, A*11, B*08, B*15, Cw*04, Cw*07 (B*15 is the current designation for earlier HLA B62 specificity).

Aysmptomatic seropositive subject, 010-063j, had a CD4+ cell count of 1150/mm3. This patient's CTL were capable of lysing autologous Gag-expressing targets but in the 15 months of this study, their HTL declined to <200/mm3. They subsequently developed pneumocystis carinii pnuemonia. [Johnson1991, PMID: 1715361].

Aysmptomatic seropositive subject, 010-115i, had a CD4+ cell count of 900/mm<sup>3</sup> and clonal cell lines, B14-restricted 115G11, 115H10, 115J7 and 115H10, were generated from the patient. [Johnson1992, PMID: 137250]. This patient's CTL were capable of lysing autologous Gag-expressing targets and remained stable without significant declines in CD4+ T cells [Johnson1991, PMID: 1715361]. The adult subject chronically infected for >13 years, was ART-naive, with a VL of 35,000 RNA copies/ml plasma and CD4+ T cells at 398 cells/ml with CTL specificity for B8 Nef. [Goulder2000b, PMID: 11120778]. Chronic subject 115I remained ART-naive at the time of publication. They had  524 and 528 CD4 cell count in cells/ml, at the time of screening and confirmation respectively. HLA typing was performed at Massachusetts General Hospital. [Altfeld2001, PMID: 11152503]. Longitudinal studies were conducted in 115i, who had been infected at least since 1987 and started HAART in 1996. Not much sequence evolution was seen in this patient [Koibuchi2005a, PMID: 15956562]. LANL database note - The HIV Sequence database lists the HLA for this patient, 115I, as A*02, A*68, B*14, B*52, Cw*08, Cw*12. (A*68 is a split antigen of the broad antigen serotype A28).

Subject 014A-001, 33 years old, who participated in a vaccine trial, acquired HIV within 376 days of administration of vaccine vCP205/gp120, 4 times. This 30 year old subject who participated in a vaccine trial acquired HIV within 67 days of administration of a placebo 4 times. Patient 04.014 was ART-naive, chronically infected, and had no clinical symptoms. In year 1, CD4+ T cell count was 697 cells/uL, plasma HIV-RNA load (pVL) was 520 copies/uL, and cell associated HIV-DNA load (cVL) was 63 copies/10ˆ3 cells. In year 3, CD4+ T cell count was 467 cells/uL, pVL was 6000 copies/uL, and cVL was 82 copies/10ˆ3 cells. In both years 1 and 3, they had a dominant response to the epitope B7-GL9 gag (353-363). [Almeida2007, PMID: 17893201]

Patient 04.034 was ART-naive, chronically infected, had no clinical symptoms, and was HLA-B*27 positive. In year 1, CD4+ T cell count was 559 cells/uL, plasma HIV-RNA load (pVL) was 200 copies/uL, and cell associated HIV-DNA load (cVL) was 35 copies/10<sup>3</sup> cells. In year 2, CD4+ T cell count was 634 cells/uL, pVL was 200 copies/uL, and cVL was 70 copies/10<sup>3</sup> cells. From years 1 to 2, the dominantly-targeted epitope sequence for B27-KK10 (263-272) changed from KRWIIMGLHK to KRWIILGLNK. This M268L mutation may account for a change in clonal dominance. [Almeida2007, PMID: 17893201]

Patient 04.054 was ART-naive, chronically infected, had no clinical symptoms, and was HLA-B*27 positive. Patient 04.064 was ART-naive, chronically infected, had no clinical symptoms, and was HLA-B*27 positive. HIV-1-infected patient 103 enrolled in a SIT study and received 8 weeks of ART (consisting of at least 3 drugs) from 84 to 92 weeks and then remained off of ART for 8 weeks. During their treatment interruption, it was found they carried the mutation for genotypic resistance to efavirenz (K170R) and so the ARV regimen was changed to zidovudine-3TC-IDV. After treatment they discontinued ART, and at week 96 the resistance mutations D76N (NRTI) and K70R (NRTI)were detected. Their mean CD4 count before, during and after SIT was respectively 581, 559, and 462, with a nadir of 246. Their mean CD8 count during and after SIT was respectively 571 and 448. Their pVL at 4 weeks off ART and mean pVL after SIT were respectively 57,794 and 21,888 HIV copies/ml. Dynamics of viral load and T cell counts are shown in Fig. 1. As the subject in this study with the poorest virological response post-SIT, 103 also developed potent HIV-specific CD8 T-cell responses!  "[Daucher2008, PMID: 18234797]. Subjects 1-05 (and identical twin 1-06) were identified within a cohort of perinatally infected children in the Los Angeles area, infected after transfusion post-premature birth. In 1990 both were seropositive and started progressive ART with poor adherence. In 2000, with late stage disease, they received a combination regimen they were adherent to. In general 1-05's viremia was greater than 1-06's along with more responses to challenge peptides. HIV sequence evolution diverged from 1995-2000 between 1-05 and 1-06, with 1-05 carrying greater sequence diversity [Yang2005a, PMID: 16306608]. See Fig. 1 for details of ART.

Non-immunized HIV-1-infected Patient 1100 from Guinea-Bissau produced cytokines in response to peptide pool Vif/Vpu A02 containing Vpu66, Vif23 and Vif101 which are supertype A*02-restricted epitopes AA9, ALVEMGHHA, SI9, SLVKHHMYI and GM9, GLADQLIHM; but the patient does not express an A02 allele.

Patient 11.007 was ART-naive, chronically infected, had no clinical symptoms, and was HLA-B*27 positive. In year 1, CD4+ T cell count was 716 cells/uL, plasma HIV-RNA load (pVL) was 4400 copies/uL, and cell-associated HIV-DNA load (cVL) was 183 copies/10<sup>3</sup> cells. In year 4, CD4+ T cell count was 494 cells/uL, pVL was 19,954 copies/uL, and cVL was 527 copies/10<sup>3</sup> cells. In both years 1 and 4, the dominantly targeted epitope was B27-KK10 gag (263-272). [Almeida2007, PMID: 17893201]

Subject 113 was HIV-seropositive and had a CD4 count 318 cells/mm<sup>3</sup> with HLA A2, A30, B27, Bw60 (aka B60, and now aka B*40). HIV-1-infected patient 114 enrolled in a SIT study and received 8 weeks of ART (consisting of at least 3 drugs) from 84 to 92 weeks and then remained off of ART for 40 weeks. HIV-1-infected patient 124 enrolled in a SIT study and received 8 weeks of ART (consisting of at least 3 drugs) from 84 to 92 weeks and then remained off of ART for 25 weeks. Their pVL at 4 weeks off ART and mean pVL after SIT were respectively 76,390 and 4,458 HIV copies/ml. Dynamics of viral load and T cell counts are shown in Fig. 1 [Daucher2008, PMID: 18234797].

Patient 127/C was shown among a large cohort of progessor patients; 127/C sera was among the most strongly neutralizing; all patients in the cohort were "presumed to be infected with clade B virus based on locations of current and former residences" [Doria-Rose2010 PMID:19923174]. Infected with HIV at least one year before VRC23 was derived; donor was ARV-experienced but had been off ARV for one year prior to blood draw used in this study; donor serum was broadly neutralizing with VRC01-like predominant specificity [Georgiev2013 PMID: 23661761]. VRC01 class [VH1-2-derived]-antibodies, in particular VRC23, was previously isolated from this donor [Zhou2015, PMID: 26004070]. Donor 127/C serum was assayed for neutralization of HIV-1 BaL; potency was higher for a Y173A-Y177A mutant of BaL [Guzzo2018 PMID: 30538178].

Subject 203 was at CDC stage B1-2 in 1993, had a mean CD4 count of 455 cells/μL, was on AZT and ddl antiviral drugs and had a history of thrush. 203 received an infusion of autologous ex vivo selectively expanded HIV-specific CTLs [Lieberman1997b, PMID: 9310470].

Subject 2030 was a 33-year-old homosexual Asian-American male with no significant past medical history who presented after 10 days of an acute illness and a history of multiple ongoing unprotected sexual encounters. At initial presentation, patient had CD4+ T cell count of 396 cell/μL and viral load of 300,000 copies of HIV RNA/mL, with a later set point of 1,000 copies/mL and stable CD4+ T cell counts >600 cells/μL without ART. This initial infection was with a HIV-1 subtype B strain that was resistant to multiple drugs. 5 mo after presentation, his CD4+ T cell count declined to 450 cells/μL and his VL increased to 35,000 copies/mL and he was found to have been superinfected with a 2nd drug-sensitive subtype B HIV-1 strain that he was unable to control [Yang2005, PMID: 15613314].
Subject 203-033, 27 years old, who participated in a vaccine trial, acquired HIV within 157 days of administration of vaccine vCP1452, 4 times. [Horton2006a; PMID: 16973582].

This 27 year old subject who participated in a vaccine trial acquired HIV within 14 days of administration of vCP1452/gp120 1 time out of a planned 4 times. Viral load was at 32,511 copies/ml 91d post-infection and response to Pol epitopes YPGIKVRQL, KIQNFRVYY, and DDTVLEEMSLPGRWK by EliSpot was 490, 365, and 215 SFC/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC respectively, after HIV-1 infection.[Horton2006a, PMID: 16973582].

Blood sample collected between 2004 & 2009 when HIV-1-infected patient 2032 was an ART-naive 27 year old had a CD4<sup>+</sup> T cell count of 500 cells/μl and a plasma VL of 13,539 copies/ml [Tarosso2017, PMID: 28594230].

Subject 204 was HIV-seropositive and had a CD4 count of 440 cells/mm3 with HLA A2, A3, B8, B62. A T cell line from the subject recognized immunodominant epitopes, RRIRQGLERILL and VWKEATTTLF [Shankar1996, PMID: 8877415]. The T cell line also recognized four epitope-containing peptides, VPVWKEATTTLFCASDAKAY, NPPIPVGEIYKRWIILGLNK, FRDYVDRFYKTLRAEQASQD and Nef 778-11 [Lieberman1995, PMID: 7742040]. Subject 204 was at CDC stage B3 in 1993, had a mean CD4 count of 185 cells/μL and had an onset of thrush 10 days before ddC antiviral treatment. 204 received an infusion of autologous ex vivo selectively expanded HIV-specific CTLs [Lieberman1997b, PMID: 9310470].

Blood sample collected between 2004 & 2009 when HIV-1-infected patient 2041 was an ART-naive 24 year old had a CD4<sup>+</sup> T cell count of 444 cells/μl and a plasma VL of 28,236 copies/ml [Tarosso2017, PMID: 28594230].

Patient serum neutralization was studied by Mikell2011 [Mikell2011, PMID:21249232] and Mikell2012 [Mikell2012, PMID: 23152926]. Subject 693-1969, infected with HIV-1 (likely subtype B) and not on ART, received triple combination monoclonal antibody therapy (PGDM1400, PGT121 and VRC07-523LS) as part of a clinical trial. 693-1969 had VLs of 8850 and 2770 RNA copies/ml, respectively, at screening and day 0 visits. Over the next 71 days, their VL was much lower or not detected before rebounding around day 85. 693-1969 was 18-65 years of age, had no history of AIDS-defining illness within previous 5 years, had not been on ART for >6 mo, had a CD4 cell count >300 cells/μ and did not have any other clinically significant acute or chronic medical condition [Julg2022, PMID: 35551291].

Project: AIEDRP (Acute Infection and Early Disease Research Program)
Note: Acutely infected Patient 8183A subsequently discontinued ART. Infection was detected in this patient 45 days following onset of symptoms; he began ART immediately and was followed longitudinally over a 4-year period. His plasma viral load (VL) became undetectable on ART (lamivudine, indinavir, nelfinavir and zalcitabine). Therapy was discontinued after 10 months when ART-related lactic acidosis developed.  He remained ART-free for >2 years until surrogate markers of disease progression necessitated reinstitution of ART [plasma viral load of 157 589 copies/ml and CD4 cell count of 322 x 10<sup>6</sup>/l 40 months post-onset of acute retroviral symptoms (POARS). At 3 months POARS, the T-cell response of 8183A was narrowly directed to CD8 T-cell epitopes in Vif and Nef. The two epitopes in Vif were determined to be restricted by HLA-B*51.  T-cell responses of 8183A at 49 months were reduced in frequency and appeared to be waning while he was on potent ART [Bansal2005a, PMID: 15718834]. Patient 8183A had a viral load of <50 copies/mL, a CD4+ T cell count of 1,000 cells/µL, and a CD4+ T cell nadir of 329 cells/µL. [Sabbaj2007, PMID: 17152014]

All immunizations were adjuvanted with 10 μg of 3M-052 in squalene emulsion (3M-052-SE) and were delivered intramuscularly and divided over the right and left quadriceps. 8229 was the source of mAbs 128_Rh and 220_Rh [Nelson2024, PMID: 39213340].

Note: Subject AC-01 was part of a pilot study for a study on STI.  His discontinuation of therapy led to recurrence of viremia after which re-institituion of therapy controlled it. An infection with HepA caused an unplanned treatment interruption after liver function abnormalities were detected. This time the HIV levels rebounded even higher but later declined to 490 RNA copies /ml plasma without any treatment, for 6d. Once liver enzymes normalized, therapy was restarted. Viral load was detected in 9d and rose to 4,680 RNA copies in 5d. Another spike in viremia was seen concomitant with an episode of bacterial pharyngitis but it declined without a reinitiation of therapy.  All this while, CTL counts remained relatively stable. [Rosenberg2000, PMID: 11029005]. Later, the patient decided to renew therapy when his VL was at 3,820 RNA copies/ml.  Acute Patient ID AC01 was 31 years of age at the time of this publication with a CD4+ cell count of 1,023/ml. at diagnosis. His HIV1/2 ELISA was negative i.e. enrolled before seroconversion. [Goulder2001a, PMID: 11148222]. AC-01 (AC01) is a symptomatic acute patient infected with B subtype R5 biologic phenotype and in whom HAART was initiated at diagnosis, with ART regimen consisting 2 NNRTIs (nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor) and a protease inhibitor - ZDV/3TC/IND, viremia was suppressed for at least 8 months prior to STI (supervised treatment interruption), with no attendant drug-induced mutations. Helper T-cell function was stimulated, and RNA copies were at 250,000/ml. at diagnosis. [Montefiori2001, PMID: 11581388].   Patient AC01's HLA were given as A*02:01, A*03, B*35.  They're VL <50 copies/ml at the time of screening and confirmation. CD4 cell count was 964 at screening and 1,025 cells/ml at the time of confirmation.  Treatment comprised 2 nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitors and 1 protease inhibitor. [Altfeld2001, PMID: 11152503]. Acute patient AC01 was given ART pre-seroconversion after a recent high risk exposure, and had a negative HIV-1 ELISA. HLA were given as A2, A3, B25, Cw4. [Altfeld2001b, PMID: 111482221]. Donor was symptomatic at time of enrollment; donor selected for the potent ELISA response of his serum to HIV-1 env gp120,  was treated early after infection and experienced several STIs, was intensively studied with respect to the generation of antibodies directed against the HIV-1 envelope glycoproteins (Montefiori et al., 2003). This individual, AC-01, was found to have a high frequency of CD4i antibodies among the Env-reactive antibodies (J. Robinson and E.S. Rosenberg, unpublished observations). Several of these CD4i antibodies are novel with respect to their heavy-chain CDR3 primary sequences, which resemble that of the CCR5 N-terminus and are unusually long, with some of their hCDR3 sequences post-translationally modified by tyrosine sulfation, as is the CCR5 N-terminus [Choe2003, PMID: 12887918]. The human monoclonal antibody 412d was isolated from an Epstein-Barr virus-transformed B-cell line, which was established from an HIV-1-infected individual (AC-01) undergoing early structured treatment interruption [Xiang2005, PMID: 15857992]. Pantophlet2008 and Dr. James Robinson state that Ab CO11 was derived from patient AC01, and it is thus added to this patient record. [Pantophlet2008, PMID: 18822440]. LANL database note - Patient AC01 of this MGH Acute cohort has the same name as a different patient, patient Record# 5777, also AC01, who was a controller in acute infection, and belongs to a different cohort of study, the AIEDRP program.

During the second treatment interruption, CTL again increased (Fig. 4d) and more epitopes were targeted [Rosenberg2000, PMID: 11029005]. Acute Patient ID AC04 of the Boston Acute Infection Cohort was 35 years of age at the time of this publication with viral load of 9,620,000 copies/ml. at diagnosis. Though presenting with symptoms, his HIV1/2 ELISA was negative i.e. enrolled before seroconversion, and ART constituted ZDV/3TC/NFV. [Goulder2001a, PMID: 11148222].  AC-04 (AC04) is an symptomatic acute patient infected with B subtype X4 biologic phenotype and in whom HAART was initiated at diagnosis, with ART regimen consisting 2 NNRTIs (nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor) and a protease inhibitor - ZDV/3TC/NFV, viremia was suppressed for at least 8 months prior to STI (supervised treatment interruption), with no attendant drug-induced mutations. Helper T-cell function was stimulated, and RNA copies were at 10,000,000/ml. [Montefiori2001, PMID: 11581388]. Since AC-04 was treated with HAART within 7 days of presentation, and the viral load was suppressed below the limit of detection within 286 days, after 617 days of successful treatment (day 624 postpresentation), AC-04 underwent four cycles of the STI mentioned above. The viral load rebounded during each treatment interruption though, resulting in a continuous decline in CD4 T cells during the periods of viral replication, reaching 237 CD4 T cells/µl at day 2,240 pp, when HAART was reinitiated. Total time on HAART was 1,410d, off 990d. [Allen2005, PMID: 16188997]. LANL database note - Patient AC04 of this MGH Acute cohort has the same name as a different patient, patient Record# 5944, also AC04, who was a controller in acute infection, and belongs to a different cohort of study, the AIEDRP program.

His CTL recognized 5 B*57-restricted epitope, ISPRTLNAW, KAFSPEVIPMF, TSTLQEQIGW, IVLPEKDSW, and HTQGYFPDWQ so that 72% of the total tested response was via B*57-restriction (Altfeld2003a).  Subject Ac-38, acutely infected with HIV-1 & not receiving ART, had first time point CD4+ count of 438 cells/μl and VL of 1,760 copies/ml. 68 days later at second time point, his CD4+ count was 588 cells/μl and VL was 1,112 copies/ml. Ac-38 was recruited from Boston Acute Infection Cohort at Massachusetts General Hospital/Fenway Community Health Ctr [Streeck2008, PMID: 18462013].

Patient AC41's HLA were given and their VL <50 and 110 copies/ml at the time of screening and confirmation. CD4 cell count was 815 cells/ml at the time of confirmation.  Treatment comprised 2 nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitors and 1 protease inhibitor. [Altfeld2001, PMID: 11152503].

ART-naive slow progressor B57-SP24 was chronically infected with HIV-1. VL and T cell counts over time are shown in Supplemental Figure 1. HLA alleles published as A*01, A*02, B*0801, B*57, Cw*0602, and Cw*07 [Koning2022, PMID: 34880106].
Plasma BB34 was from HIV-1-infected blood donors identified by the South African National Blood Service in Johannesburg; the sample was collected between 2002 and 2003 [Gray2009a PMID: 19692477].

ART-naive controller BCN07, chronically infected with HIV, had a CD4 count of 1,144 cells/μL & VL of 380 copies/ml [Yang2021, PMID: 33979627].

Infant was detected HIV-positive at 114 days, suggesting transmission by breastfeeding. Infection was clade A. bnAbs (e.g., BF520.1) were isolated at 15 months of age [Simonich2016 PMID: 27345369]. Infant plasma had neutralization activity in samples from 15 through 27 months of age [Goo2014 PMID:24859529].
BG376 was an infant with serum neutralization in the top quartile among 28 infants; HIV-1 diagnosis (subtype C) at 6 months of age [Goo2014 PMID: 24859529].

This infant was infected at birth from mother, MG505, or during breastfeeding; their first positive test result was at 6 weeks of age [Wu2006a, PMID:16378985].  BG505 was the infant of a pair from antiretroviral-naive mother-infant pairs enrolled in the Nairobi Breastfeeding Trial and had great neutralization breadth [Goo2014, PMID: 24859529]. BG505 was a clade-A HIV-1-infected infant born in 1994 and sampled several times up through 27 months of age (May 1996). BG505's Env has been studied extensively, previously, - BG505 is the source of Clade A BG505.SOSIP Env immunogen, the first high-resolution structural characterization of a native-like Env trimer was theirs, conformational states of Env and its function in receptor binding were delineated, trimer glycosylation, and the antigenic landscape of Env were studied, it was used to generate more biologically relevant simian-human immunodeficiency virus chimeras (SHIVs) and, pre-clinical vaccine trials are being conducted based on this Env immunogen [Simonich2021, PMID: 34195680].
BH217 was an infant with serum neutralization in the top quartile among 28 infants; HIV-1 diagnosis (subtype A) at 4 months of age [Goo2014 PMID: 24859529].

Patient ID NM07 was a 28 year old C-clade-infected, asymptomatic, ART-naive individual from South Africa with CD4 count of 1437 cells/µl, viral load (VL) of 740 copies/ml, and who had an immune response to peptides from the Zembe2011 study.  [Zembe2011; PMID: 22022524].
BN469 was an infant with serum neutralization in the top quartile among 28 infants; HIV-1 diagnosis (subtype A) at 3.7 months of age [Goo2014 PMID: 24859529].
Adult subject chronically infected for 11 years, ART-naive, with VL 2,000 RNA copies/ml plasma with CTL specificity for A2 Gag. [Goulder2000b, PMID: 11120778].

Project: Genitourinary Medicine Clinic at the Medical Research Council (MRC) Unit in The Gambia
Note: BOH1-001 was an asymptomatic, ART-naive, HIV-1-infected participant with 4.15 copies of HIV-1 RNA/ml, CD4 count of 330 cells/µl and a cumulative magnitude of EliSpot against Gag peptide pools of 415 SFU/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC.

BORI or Patient3 infected at age 25, presented to University of Alabama at Birmingham hospital. 15 and 30 days after onset of symptoms, CD4 count was 931 and 1,126 cells/ml respectively [Clark1991, PMID: 1900576]. BORI established high persisting VL (186,305 HIV-1 RNA copies/ml at 218 DFOSx) and had a steep decline in CD4 count within 2 yrs to 393 CD4 cells/ml [Jones2004, PMID: 15545352]. At CDC stage 1 of infection, there were 817 CD4+ cells/ml, and 2.4x10<sup>6</sup> HIV-1 RNA copies/ml.  [Piatak1993, PMID: 8096089]. Initial HLA serotyping from Borrow 1994 and infectious virus was first cleared from plasma only 14d after onset of acute retroviral syndrome, with reappearance in plasma at day 542 [Borrow1994, PMID: 8057491]. Data from this patient is also synonymous with Datasets 15/16/17/18. Rate of escape of four reported CTL escape variants was estimated from longitudinal data from Jones2004. The patient was in primary infection when samples were collected. [Asquith2006, PMID: 16515366].

Subject BR3 was the older brother to monozygotic twins TW1 and TW2 by three years. TW1 and TW2 shared needles with the same HIV+ person at the same time and later shared needles with BR3, so that all 3 brothers were infected by the same viral strain, sharing similar viral loads, but CD4+ decline was more rapid in BR3. BR3 had negative HIV-1 ELISA in December 1999 but a seropositive result in November 2000, seroconverting 13 months later than his twin brothers. He was HCV co-infected and ART-naive, [Draenert2006, PMID: 16533886].
BT326 was an infant with serum neutralization in the top quartile among 28 infants; HIV-1 diagnosis (subtype A) at birth [Goo2014 PMID: 24859529].

Subject C-01 was a longitudinally studied child and was between the ages of 2.02 and 12.7 years, having acquired HIV via vertical transmission. C-01 started zidovudine treatments at 3 months old and had a mono-specific anti-HIV response at 1 year old. The patient subsequently dropped CD4 counts to undetectable, and was then administered triple combination therapy including a protease inhibitor which increased CD4 counts and temporarily decreased viremia.  At this point a Pol-specific response returned as well as a de novo Gag 24-specific response to epitope KAFSPEVIPMF was detected. Viremia containment however, was now not sustained, increasing to 1105 copies/mL., and to contain it his therapy was changed to a combination of saquinavir, ritonavir, and stavudine, which resulted in a rapid decrease in plasma viremia to below detection, but now all HIV-specific CTL response also became undetectable. This patient is a sibling to female Subject C-02 who has the same HLA as C-01. [Spiegel1999, PMID: 10395850].

Patient C01 was a 32 year old Chronic Controller who was tested at at least two time points, with CD4 count going from 538 to 462 cells/µL and viral load from 4.2 to 5.9 cp/mL. [Roider2013, PMID: 24312453].

Subject C-02 was a longitudinally studied child and was between the ages of 2.02 and 12.7 years, having acquired HIV via vertical transmission. C-02 had no anti-HIV specific activity as a neonate, but developed a broader response after treatment. When nevirapine and zidovudine were added to dideoxyinosine treatment, the plasma viral load in patient C-02 decreased to below detection, and her CTL response decreased to low levels. However, when dual drug therapy was re-instituted, plasma viremia rose, and so did her HIV-1–specific CTL response.  This patient is a sibling to male Subject C-01 who has the same HLA as C-02. [Spiegel1999, PMID: 10395850].

Patient C02 was a 46 year old Chronic Controller who was tested at at least two time points, with CD4 count going from 592 to 522 cells/µL and viral load from 4.1 to 2.4 cp/mL. [Roider2013, PMID: 24312453].

Subject C-03 was a longitudinally studied child and was between the ages of 2.02 and 12.7 years, having acquired HIV via vertical transmission. C-03 had no anti-HIV specific activity as a neonate, but developed a broader response even before combination therapy was begun. At 9 months C-03 had >4% of HIV-1-specific CTL as B8-Nef recognizing. Viral load reached its set point between 2 and 3 years of life, while the patient was receiving dual therapy with dideoxyinosine and zidovudine. Later, the gradual decrease of viremia is paralleled by the decrease in anti-Nef-specific CTL response. [Spiegel1999, PMID: 10395850].

Patient C03 was a 32 year old Chronic Controller who was tested at at least two time points, with CD4 count going from 785 to 761 cells/µL and viral load from 3.7 to 8.3 cp/mL. [Roider2013, PMID: 24312453].

Subject C-04 was a longitudinally studied child and was between the ages of 2.02 and 12.7 years, having acquired HIV via vertical transmission. C-04 developed a CTL HIV-specific response even before combination therapy was begun. C-04 had low CTL, low viral load, low CD4 counts. CD4 counts increased after combination therapy, but not CTL response. [Spiegel1999, PMID: 10395850].

Patient C04 was a 56 year old Chronic Controller who was tested at at least two time points, with CD4 count going from 517 to 543 cells/µL and viral load from 2.3 to 5.6 cp/mL. [Roider2013, PMID: 24312453].

Subject C-05 was a longitudinally studied child and was between the ages of 2.02 and 12.7 years, having acquired HIV via vertical transmission. No CTL response was initially detected in C-05. When therapy was changed to nelfinavir, lamivudine, and stavudine, there was an initial drop in viral load, but viral rebound occurred, at which time an HIV-1–specific CTL response was detected. When saquinavir and ritonavir were substituted for nelfinavir, both plasma viremia and the CTL response dropped. [Spiegel1999, PMID: 10395850].

Subject C-06 was a longitudinally studied child and was between the ages of 2.02 and 12.7 years, having acquired HIV via vertical transmission. Patient C-06 was unusual in having no detectable HIV-1–specific CTL pre-combination therapy, despite CD4 T-cell counts >11000/mL. When therapy was changed to nelfinavir, lamivudine, and stavudine, there was an initial drop in viral load, but viral rebound occurred, at which time an HIV-1–specific CTL response was detected. When saquinavir and ritonavir were substituted for nelfinavir, both plasma viremia and the CTL response dropped. [Spiegel1999, PMID: 10395850].

Subject C-07 was a longitudinally studied child and was between the ages of 2.02 and 12.7 years, having acquired HIV via vertical transmission. Patient C-07 did make HIV-1–specific CTL responses pre-combination therapy. Addition of protease inhibitor to the drug regimen was followed by a reduced viremia which accompanied a decline in HIV-specific CTL, but after a year strong Pol- and moderate Nef-specific CTL responses were detected. [Spiegel1999, PMID: 10395850].

Project: Cohort 2, subset of NSW (transmission acquired HIV) TAHIV study
Note: Long term survivor (LTS) Study subject C105 was a 49-year old female infected for 16 years and with a viral load of <200, CD4 and CD8 counts of 1,000 x 10<sup>6</sup> and 950 x 10<sup>6</sup>; carrying wild type alleles for chemokine receptors CCR5 and CCR2. Note: LTNP Study subject C135 was a 53-year old male infected for 18 years and with a viral load of <200, CD4 and CD8 counts of 528 x 10<sup>6</sup> and 496 x 10<sup>6</sup>; carrying wild type alleles for chemokine receptors CCR5 and CCR2, except for one Δ32 allele of CCR5 (32 bp deletion). [Wang2002b, PMID: 12504566].   Elite controller C135 has maintained undetectable vPL and steady CD4 cell count without treatment after infection with attenuated nef/LTR-deleted HIV-1, for decades, likely due to two protective HLA alleles (B57 and DRB1*13:02:01), heterozygous CCR5 Δ32 genotype and vigorous p24-stimulated PBMC proliferation. [Zaunders2019, PMID: 31191910]. C135 was a member of the Sydney Blood Bank Cohort. His Δnef infecting HIV-1 strain, Δ32/wt CR5, and protective B*57 HLA allele were viral and host genetic factors associated with slow disease progression. HLA alleles reported as A1, A33, B50, B57. [Dyer2008, PMID: 19077215]

Note: C18 was a member of the Sydney Blood Bank Cohort, infected at age 70 by blood transfusion from donor D36 (patient record #6299). [Learmont1999, PMID: 10352163].

C92 had the same HIV+ blood transfusion donor as subjects C42 and C76. [Geczy2000; PMID: 10717811].

Project: Sydney Blood Bank Cohort (SBBC), subset of NSW TAHIV study
Note: LTNP Study subject C98 was a 61-year old male infected for 17 years and with a viral load of 990, CD4 and CD8 counts of 527 x 10<sup>6</sup> and 901 x 10<sup>6</sup>; carrying wild type alleles for chemokine receptors CCR5 and CCR2. C98 had the same HIV+ blood transfusion donor, D36 (patient record #6299), as subjects C49, C54, C64 and C135. [Geczy2000; PMID: 10717811]. C98 was a member of the Sydney Blood Bank Cohort. Born on 11July, 1937, and infected at age 44 via blood transfusion on 1January, 1982, he lost LTNP status when he began ART on 17September, 1999, in response to disease progression associated with the emergence of divergent strains preceding viral increases and changes in immune response. He died from non-AIDS related causes at age 64. His Δnef infecting HIV-1 strain was a viral genetic factor associated with slow disease progression, and his R/R polymorphism of FcγRIIA was associated with increased disease progression. He weakly responded to multiple pools containing epitopes in the reverse transcriptase protein. HLA reported as A2, A28, B7, B60. [Dyer2008, PMID: 19077215]. LANL database note - this patient is not linked with any CTL epitope record i.e. they have not shown immunogenic response to any database-annotated, tested epitope.

Participant CAP45 was a controller who was sampled 5 wpi (weeks post infection) on 11May2005, VL (viral load) = 236,000 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 974 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. CAP045 was defined as an early controller. Total maximum EliSpot assay response was 11,400 SFU/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC, with a breadth of recognizing 8 peptide pools (other subject responses ranged from 0 to 11 peptide pools recognized, with an average of 4-7 pools recognized) [Gray2009, PMID: 18945774]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP45 was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. Subject CAP045 was first positive for vRNA at 45 years with clade-C HIV-1, Fiebig Stage I/II, and virus setpoint 871 copies/ml. At Fiebig Stage I/II, CD4+ count was 306 cells/µl, last sequencing being performed 440 and 636 days after then. At Fiebig Stage I/II, CD4+ count was >350 cells/µl in the absence of ART [Abrahams2013, PMID:23370465]. CAP45's complete T-cell response was examined using peptides composed of autologous founder sequence and subsequent evolutionary variants [Liu2013a; PMID: 23221345].  Participant CAP45 mounted a response against the V1V2 and C3-V4 regions of Env [Moore2008, PMID: 18057243]. CAP45 had a VL setpoint of 556 RNA copies/ml one year pi [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996].

Rapid progressing participant CAP063 was sampled 4 wpi (weeks post infection) on 19Jan2005, VL (viral load) = 277,000 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 414 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP063 was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. Subject CAP63 was first positive for vRNA at 32 years with clade-C HIV-1, Fiebig Stage III, and virus setpoint >10,000 copies/ml.  At Fiebig Stage I/II, CD4+ count was <350 cells/ul within the first year of infection [Abrahams2013, PMID: 23370465]. CAP63 progressed rapidly to AIDS and entered treatment at 37 wpi, but their polyclonal sera showed evidence of autologous neutralization against the C3-V4 region of Env [Moore2008, PMID: 18057243].

CAP065 was defined as a rapid progressor. Total maximum EliSpot assay response was 5,950 SFU/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC, with a breadth of recognizing 11 peptide pools (other subject responses ranged from 0 to 11 peptide pools recognized, with an average of 4-7 pools recognized). [Gray2009, PMID: 18945774]

CAP069 was defined as a rapid progressor. Total maximum EliSpot assay response was 560 SFU/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC, with a breadth of recognizing 3 peptide pools (other subject responses ranged from 0 to 11 peptide pools recognized, with an average of 4-7 pools recognized). [Gray2009, PMID: 18945774]

Subject CAP85 first positive for vRNA at 24 years with clade-C HIV-1, Fiebig Stage V [Abrahams2013, PMID: 23370465]. Participant CAP85 was from a sex worker cohort and was sampled 5 wpi (weeks post infection) on 22Jun2005, VL (viral load) = 621,000 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 419 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP85 was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. This participant's viral sequences carried significant APOBEC3G-driven hypermutation [Abrahams2009, PMID: 19193811]. Data from CAP85 is from the first 12 months of infection. She had a confirmed peptide response to the presumed optimal epitope within peptide Nef(113-131). She experienced autologous viral escape at 18.5 weeks, and loss of response at 26 weeks. [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

CAP174 is a rapid progressor from the CAPRISA 002 acute infection study in Durban, South Africa. She was infected with a subtype C virus. Data from this patient is from the first 12 months of infection. She had a confirmed peptide response to the presumed optimal epitope within peptide Nef(73-87). Autologous viral escape occurred at 11 weeks. [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

Donor CAP 177 was infected with two different HIV-1 viruses, CAP177-4C virus which contains a long V1 loop and the CAP177-5D virus which has a short V1 loop [vanDorsten2020, PMID: 31619559]. CAP177 had a VL setpoint of 18,000 RNA copies/ml one year pi and her sera targeted the C3V4 and V1V2 regions of Env [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996].

Donor CAP206 chronically infected at time of antibody CAP206-CH12 derivation [Morris2011, PMID: 21980336]; antibody neutralization profile studied since point of seroconversion on 15Jun2005; infected with subtype C, participant was sampled 8 wpi (weeks post infection) on 12Jul2005, VL (viral load) = 368,000 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 365 cells/µl.  [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP206 was at Fiebig stage VI [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. Polyclonal neutralization was mediated by anti-MPER antibodies [Gray2009a, PMID: 19692477] for this broad neutralizer [Moody2012a, PMC: 3442051]. CAP206 was defined as a rapid progressor. Total maximum EliSpot assay response was 8,040 SFU/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC, with a breadth of recognizing 10 peptide pools (other subject responses ranged from 0 to 11 peptide pools recognized, with an average of 4-7 pools recognized) [Gray2009, PMID: 18945774]. With a breadth of neutralization of 47%, and a VL set point of 315,000 at 12 months, antibody targets were known to be against MPER and C3V4 [Mabvakure2019, PMID: 30567996]. Transmitted founder (TF) variants of HIV from CAP206 when injected into <i>Rhesus macaques<i/> generated the DH427 nAb clonal lineage [Bradley2016a, PMID: 27612593]. Participant CAP206 mounted a response against the V1V2 region of Env [Moore2008, PMID: 18057243]. Data from CAP206 is from the first 12 months of infection. She had a confirmed peptide response to the presumed optimal epitope within peptide Nef(129-147). She had no evidence of escape and no loss of response for the epitope. HLA reported as A*0301/3201; B*0702/4403; Cw*0210/0702. [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

Subject CAP210 first positive for vRNA at 43 years with clade-C HIV-1 was sampled 5 wpi (weeks post infection) on 25May2005, VL (viral load) = 127,000 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 461 cells/µl. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP210 was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. In 12 months this patient still had weak nAb responses, and progressed rapidly [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164], Fiebig Stage I/II, and virus setpoint 295,121 copies/ml.  At Fiebig Stage I/II, CD4+ count was 306 cells/µl, last sequencing being performed 807 days after then [Liu2013a, PMID:23221345]. Data from CAP210 is from the first 12 months of infection. She had a confirmed peptide response to the presumed optimal epitope within peptide p15(290-321). She showed no evidence of autologous viral escape for the epitope. [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686] CAP210 was defined as a rapid progressor. Total maximum EliSpot assay response was 2,850 SFU/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC, with a breadth of recognizing 6 peptide pools (other subject responses ranged from 0 to 11 peptide pools recognized, with an average of 4-7 pools recognized) [Gray2009, PMID: 18945774]
Subtype-C positive participant studied during acute/early infection. [Chopera2011, PMID: 21613398].

CAP222 was defined as an early controller. Total maximum EliSpot assay response was 2,970 SFU/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC, with a breadth of recognizing 5 peptide pools (other subject responses ranged from 0 to 11 peptide pools recognized, with an average of 4-7 pools recognized). [Gray2009, PMID: 18945774]

CAP225 is a progressor from the CAPRISA 002 acute infection study in Durban, South Africa. She was infected with a subtype C virus and carried the protective A*81:01 allele. Data from this patient is from the first 12 months of infection. She had a confirmed peptide response to 2 peptides in Nef (Nef(125-139) and Nef(161-179)) and a peptide in p24. Autologous viral escape for the presumed optimal epitope in Nef(125-139) and for the Nef(161-179) peptide occurred at 1.5 weeks and 28.5 weeks, and response was lost at 29 and 33 weeks, respectively. She did not show evidence of autologous viral escape for the presumed optimal epitope in p24. [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

Controller participant CAP228 enrolled into this study [vanEeden2018, PMID: 30540944] following infection with a HIV-1 subtype C virus in March 2005 and was sampled 7 wpi (weeks post infection) on 11May2005, VL (viral load) = 3,840 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 867 cells/µl. She was initially identified as a virological controller, having a CD4 count of > 350 cells/mL and a viral load of < 2000 copies/mL on at least two consecutive measurements after 6 months of infection [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP228 was sampled on 18May2005, VL (viral load) = 2,360 copies/ml, CD4 count = 851 cells/µl, and was at Fiebig stage Vi [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. CAP228 had a VL setpoint of 1,520 RNA copies/ml one year pi and her sera targeted the V1V2 and C3V4 regions of Env [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996]. However, antiretroviral therapy was started in June 2012 following a peak viral load of 228,207 RNA copies/mL at 370 wpi. HLA data is from Chopera et al. [Chopera2008, PMID: 18369479]. Data from CAP228 is from the first 12 months of infection. She had confirmed peptide responses to the presumed optimal epitopes within peptides p24(163-179) and Nef(129-147). She showed no evidence of autologous viral escape for either epitope. HLA reported by Mlotshwa2010 as HLA-A*2301/2638; B*4403/5101; Cw*0303/0701.  [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP229 was sampled on 19July2005, VL (viral load) = 126,000 copies/ml, CD4 count = 558 cells/µl, and was at Fiebig stage ND [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. Subtype-C positive participant studied during acute/early infection. [Chopera2011, PMID: 21613398]. CAP229 had a VL setpoint of 24,600 RNA copies/ml one year pi and her sera targeted the V1V2 region of Env [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996].

Progressing Participant CAP239 is subtype-C infected, and was sampled 5 wpi (weeks post infection) on 10Aug2005, VL (viral load) = 95,800 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 845 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP239 was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. Her first positive for vRNA was at 44 years with clade-C HIV-1, Fiebig Stage I/II, and virus setpoint 26,915 copies/ml.  At Fiebig Stage I/II, CD4+ count was 792 cells/µl, last sequencing being performed 807 days after then. [Liu2013a, 23221345].  CAP239's HLA is reported as A*01:23, A*29:02, B*42:01, B*58:01, C*06:02, C*17:01 elsewhere [Chopera2011; PMID: 21613398].  Participant CAP239 mounted a response against the V1V2 region of Env [Moore2008, PMID: 18057243].

HIV-1 subtype C-infected progressing participant CAP244 was from a seroincidence cohort and was sampled 8 wpi (weeks post infection) on 23May2005, VL (viral load) = 19,200 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 557 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP244 was at Fiebig stage VI [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270].

Infected with subtype C. HLA data from Chopera 2008 [PMID 18369479]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women,  CAP248 was sampled on 24May2005, VL (viral load) = 55,000 copies/ml, CD4 count = 420 cells/µl, and was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. CAP248 first developed cross-neutralizing antibodies after one year of infection, and mAb CAP248-2B was isolated at 3.5 years post-infection [Wibmer2017, PMID: 28076415]. CAP248 had a VL setpoint of 64,600 RNA copies/ml one year pi and her sera targeted the gp120-gp41 interface region of Env [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996].

HLA data from Chopera 2008 [PMID 18369479]. Subtype-C-infected Donor CAP255 was a progressor from a seroincidence and not a sex-worker cohort, and was sampled 8 wpi (weeks post infection) on 21Jun2005, VL (viral load) = 196,000 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 693 cells/µl  [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP255 was at Fiebig stage VI [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. CAP255 had a VL setpoint of 18,200 RNA copies/ml one year pi and her sera targeted the C3V4 region of Env [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996]. Sequencing data indicated that CAP255 was co-infected by three related viral variants, all of which had an intact N332 glycan, which persisted in >90% of later viruses [Kitchen2019 https://doi.org/10.1093/ve/vez002]. Data from CAP255 is from the first 12 months of infection. She had confirmed peptide responses to the presumed optimal epitopes within peptides Nef(129-147) and Nef(101-119). Autologous viral escape from Nef(129-147) occurred at 24.5 weeks, and response was lost at 51 weeks. She showed no evidence of autologous viral escape for Nef(101-119). [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

Donor 256 was a progressor infected with subtype C who was sampled 6 wpi (weeks post infection) on 05Sep2005, VL (viral load) = 56,500 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 689 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP256 was at Fiebig stage VI [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]; superinfected 13 to 15 weeks after infection with a second unrelated subtype C virus; diagnosed with extrapulmonary tuberculosis in November 2009 and started on ARV January 2010, 4 years 7 months postinfection; showed high level of cross-neutralizing antibody activity at 3 years of infection. Enrolled in CAPRISA Acute Infection study (CAP 002). [Moore2011, PMID: 24270156]; [Doria-Rose2014, PMID: 24590074]; [Yin2021, PMID: 34261793]. Escape viruses from Ab CAP256.09 were also found in this patient, [Andrabi2015, PMID: 26588781]. Known to be super-infected, CAP256 had a VL setpoint of 178,000 one year pi and her sera targeted the V1V2 region of Env [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996]. CAP256 mounted a potent V2-specific bnAb response, expressing persistently high levels of plasma IgG3, increasing over time, which mediated both broad neutralizing activity and potent Fc function (ADCC, ADCD, ADCP, and ADCT), [Richardson2019a, PMID: 31841557]. Data from CAP256 is from the first 12 months of infection. She had a confirmed peptide response to the presumed optimal epitope within Nef(77-95). She experienced initial nonrecognition of the peptide (most likely due to sequence variation in the flanking region and within the epitope), had autologous viral escape at 21 weeks, and experienced peak IFN-γ at 34 weeks post-infection. [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women,  CAP257 was sampled on 12Sept2005, VL (viral load) = 276,000 copies/ml, CD4 count = 450 cells/µl, and was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. Clade-C infected. HLA and other patient data from LANL HIV Sequence database [Wibmer2016, PMID: 27581986]. CAP257 had a VL setpoint of 8,260 RNA copies/ml one year pi and her sera targeted the V1V2 region of Env and the CD4 binding site [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996]. Data from CAP257 is from the first 12 months of infection. CAP257 was defined as a progressor. She had confirmed peptide responses to the presumed optimal epitopes within peptides p17(9-26) and Nef(129-147). She showed no evidence of autologous viral escape and no loss of response for either epitope. [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

CAP37 is a rapid progressor from the CAPRISA 002 acute infection study in Durban, South Africa. She was infected with a subtype C virus. Response to the presumed optimal epitope within Nef(6-24) was lost at 13 weeks. Autologous viral escape for Nef(129-147) occurred at 9.5 weeks, and response was lost at 15 weeks. Donor CAP061 was a HIV-1 subtype C-infected participant from a seroincidence and not a sex-worker cohort. CAP61 was a Controller despite weak nAb responses at 12 months. She was sampled 8 wpi (weeks post infection) on 20Dec2004, VL (viral load) = 610 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 389 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP61 was at Fiebig stage VI [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]

HIV-1 subtype C-infected participant CAP8 was sampled 3 wpi (weeks post infection). On 17May2005, VL (viral load) = 373,040 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 360 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. CAP008 was defined as a rapid progressor. Total maximum EliSpot assay response was 9,165 SFU/10<sup>6</sup> PBMC, with a breadth of recognizing 9 peptide pools (other subject responses ranged from 0 to 11 peptide pools recognized, with an average of 4-7 pools recognized) [Gray2009, PMID: 18945774]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP8 was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. This participant's viral sequences showed evidence of cellular immune pressure with a mutation in a HLA-B*0801-restricted epitope [Abrahams2009, PMID: 19193811]. CAP8 had a VL setpoint of 39,300 RNA copies/ml one year pi [Mabvakure2019, PMID:30567996]. Data from CAP8 is from the first 12 months of infection. CAP8 was defined as a rapid progressor. She had confirmed peptide responses to the presumed optimal epitopes within peptides p17(9-26) and Nef(101-119). Autologous viral escape occurred at 11.5 weeks for both, and response was lost for p17(9-26) at 30 weeks.  [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

Progressing HIV-1 subtype C-infected participant CAP84 was sampled 3 wpi (weeks post infection) on 28Feb2005, VL (viral load) = 9,140 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 363 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP84 was at Fiebig stage V [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. Participant CAP84 did not generate any anti-V1V2 polyclonals but did have autologous neutralization activity against the C3-V4 region of Env [Moore2008, PMID: 18057243].

HIV-1 subtype C-infected participant CAP88 was from a seroincidence cohort and was sampled 5 wpi (weeks post infection) on 17Feb2005, VL (viral load) = 29,400 RNA copies/ml and CD4 count = 963 cells/µl [Gray2007, PMID: 17409164], [Gray2011, PMID: 21613396]. Part of the CAPRISA002 cohort of 20 women, CAP88 was at Fiebig stage VI [Treurnicht2010, PMID: 19913270]. With a breadth of neutralization of 8%, and a VL set point of 38,700 at 12 months, antibody targets were against C3V4 [Mabvakure2019, PMID: 30567996]. The potent nAbs against C3 drove viral escape [Scheepers2020, PMID: 33238131]. Isotypes IgG1, IgG2 and IgGA1 were all isolated from this participant [Moyo-Gwete2022, PMID: 36182959]. Participant CAP88 mounted a response against the V1V2 and C3-V4 regions of Env [Moore2008, PMID: 18057243]. Data from CAP88 is from the first 12 months of infection. She had confirmed peptide responses to the presumed optimal epitopes within p15(357-374) and Nef(113-131). She experienced transient autologous viral escape in the flanking region of the epitope within p15(357-374) at 10 weeks, resulting in a decrease of responses, until response was lost at 26 weeks. Though she experienced a change back from valine to alanine at position 374 between weeks 26 and 54, there was no detectable IFN-γ ELISPOT during this time. She showed no evidence of autologous viral escape for the epitope within Nef(113-131). [Mlotshwa2010, PMID: 20826686]

Donor CBJC263 is an HIV-1 B′ chronic infected patient of Fuyang city in Anhui Province, central China. He was infected through unregulated commercial plasma donation during 1992–1995 (Su et al., 2003) and was naive to ART at the time of sampling (Hu et al., 2012). Whole blood samples were collected in 2005 and 2007 [Hu2023 PMID: 36577292].

Diagnosed in the early 1990s with an HIV-1 subtype B infection through plasma donation. The patient was followed every 6 months as part of a cohort to investigate HIV-1 disease progression correlates, including innate immunity, CTL responses, and neutralizing antibodies. As a member of this cohort and after 15 years of infection without antiviral treatment, CBJC438 displayed a CD4+ T cell count ranging from 345 to 827 cells/μl with a viral load from 18,700 to 225,000 copies/ml during 2005 to 2012 [Kumar2020, PMID: 32938661].

CBJC504 became infected with the clade B strain around 1995. He remained therapy naive before the blood samples were collected in 2006, and maintained CD4 T-cell counts above 300 cell/uL at all times. He was identified as a cross-reactive neutralizer, for his plasma showed neutralization activity against HIV-1 BG505 (clade A), JR-CSF (clade B), and HIV-2 C1 (HIV-1 MPER) chimeras. From 2006 to 2009, the subject had steady CD4 T-cell counts, ranging from 335 to 394 cell/ L, and the viral load slightly increased from 20,700 copies/mL in 2006 to 58,100 copies/mL in 2009. Plasma samples collected from 2005 to 2010, for a total of 6 time points, were analyzed for neutralization sensitivity. Env gene sequences were derived from the plasma samples collected in 2006 and 2009. [Tang2023 PMID: 36986419]. Patient classified as a slow progressor on the basis of above information [LANL database]. CBJC504 viral sequences were published, and the patient's risk factor was mentioned as being nosocomial through an unregulated commercial plasma donation [Fu et al. 2024 PMID:38643858].

At the time mAbs B2 - B7 were derived (2005 plasma sample), the donor was 44 years old and had not received antiviral therapy. Infected by clade B' obtained through non-sterile medical practices, probably 1992-1995. Described as an elite neutralizer, based on serum neutralization [Li2016 PMID: 26671829]. The patient's serum neutralization and Env clone sequences were studied at 3 timepoints (2005, 2006, 2008); due to the progression of his disease, he began ARV treatment in 2009 [Hu2021 PMID: 33805985]. Pseudoviruses from CBJC515 sequences were resistant to PGT135 [Hu2025 PMID: 40559564].

Elite neutralizer macaque CE8J demonstrated broad & potent neutralizing activity after infection with SHIV<sub>AD8</sub>, but succumbed to immunodeficiency when a neutralization-resistent SHIV variant emerged and was euthanized at 117 weeks post-infection [Walker2011a, PMID: 22123961].

Caucasian male CH40 was a nucleic acid test positive, seronegative, Fiebig Stage II - 56 year old at screening for enrollment, with a viral set point of 13,183 RNA copies per ml and CD4+ median count of 990 cells/µL. He self-reported as a male having sex with male and no history of intravenous drug use. Hey experienced a period of falling viral load and rapid escapes within 35 days of screening, followed by an increased T cell response dominated by Rev 49-66-specific response, and then a strong response to an invariant Pol 824-841 epitope. [Goonetilleke2009, PMID: 19487423].  Subject CH040 first positive for vRNA at 56 years with clade-B HIV-1, Fiebig Stage I/II, and virus setpoint 14,791 copies/ml. At Fiebig Stage I/II, CD4+ count was 986 cells/µl, last sequencing being performed 1187 days after then [Liu2013a; PMID:23221345].  pVL was 7,580 copies/mL at set point between 36 and 56 weeks [Ferrari2011; PMID: 21347345]. [Wagh2018a, PMID: 30355496]. Evolution of the T/F Env within CH040 went from partially-closed to closed over more than 1000 days, with the ability to be neutralized decreasing for many Abs tested [Parthasarathy2024, PMID: 39187497].

Patient CH9105 was a lactating mother who did not post-natally transmit HIV to her infant; her breast milk VL was <240 copies/ml, peripheral blood CD4+ T cell count was 266/ml, and viral load was 5207/ml at 4-6 weeks postpartum. [Jeffries2016, PMID: 26242599]. Sacha2015 [PMID: 25100291] also identify patient CH9105 as NOT transmitting HIV-1 to the infant during breastfeeding. However, later papers indicate that she did transmit the virus prenatally. Marichannegowda2021 [PMID: 34337555] analyzed sequences from a mother-infant pair identified as Pair5_9112, and deposited sequences to GenBank using patient code 9112m, likely coded for convenience to match with the infant 9112i; they estimated that the viral transmission occurred 19 days before birth, based on the date of infection estimated from the infant sequences. Martinez2020 [PMID: 32156815] also analyzed sequences from mother-infant pair 9105-9112, and the sequences used for 9105 match identically to the 9112m sequences deposited by Marichannegowda2021, hence the assignment of the patient alias 9112m with reasonable confidence. Martinez2020 [PMID: 32156815] provide clinical data that match previous studies for this patient (CD4 = 266/ml, VL=5207/ml), and document that 9105 maternal plasma bnAb responses targeting the V3 glycan supersite may have selected for an N332 V3 glycan neutralization-resistant infant-T/F virus.

Project: Sydney Blood Bank Cohort (SBBC), subset of NSW TAHIV study
Note: D36 was the blood donor for 8 recipients of HIV-1 infections in the Sydney Blood Bank Cohort. He was infected with a subtype B dual tropic R5X4 virus. CD4+ T cell counts remained stable from 1984 to 1995, then declined to 160 cells/uL in February 1996. Viral load increased from 1,100 copies/mL in August 1995 to 20,800 copies/mL in June 1998. In December 1998 he was diagnosed with HIV associated dementia, and in January 1999 he was placed on HAART (abacavir, nevirapine, zidovudine). CD4+ T cell count after HAART initiation recovered to >600 cells/uL. [Churchill2004, PMID: 15551218]  D36 was found to be infected with a strain of HIV-1 (subtype B) with a conserved deletion of 150 or more base pairs (bp) in the nef–LTR overlap region and with duplications and rearrangements of nuclear factor kB (NFkB) and Sp1 transcription factor binding sites in the LTR. [Learmont1999, PMID:10352163]. As of 2001, changes had been occurring for the last 5 years in the D36 nef/LTR sequences. The most recently evolved quasispecies of the D36 virus had larger deletions from the nef/LTR region and increased viral fitness. [Birch2001, PMID: 11564591] In September 2004, he was placed on a different HAART routine (abacavir, nevirapine, lamivudine). [Gray2007b, PMID: 17634131]. LANL database note - this patient is not linked with any CTL epitope record i.e. they have not shown immunogenic response to any database-annotated, tested epitope.
At the time MAb HGT4 was derived, patient was age 33, infected 5.5 years since diagnosis, and had received HAART. Infected with clade CRF01  [Corti2010 PMID: 20098712].

This patient, a daughter, was perinatally infected by her mother, Mother 11 in a different paper. They were both studied for MTCT. The mother's viral isolate, M2, was transmitted vertically to her daughter who carries viral isolate D2. The daughter was born infected in 1984 but asymptomatic until 1995 with subtle neurological deterioration and poor performance at school and so also began ART in 1995. Details of CD4 and viremic levels over the years are given in Table 1 of Reinis2007 [Reinis2007, PMID: 17331038]. Full-genome RNA sequences were 1993-DI, code WCM32P0793:DQ487188 and 1995-D2, code WCM32P1195: DQ487189. As of 2007, this patient was healthy and had a healthy child who was not infected. Patient, Daughter1, was homozygous for the wild-type CCR5 gene like her mother, and shared HLA alleles A19, B44 and Cw16 She had a younger sister, birthed from the same mother, who was not infected in utero.

Long-term non-progressor, HIV-1-infected volunteer Donor 005 first tested positive in January 1986 with a CD4 count of 695 cells/µl, VL of 64,458 cell/ml. [Papagno2002, PMID: 12452843]. With a CD4 count of 728, and a VL of 38,219, 51% of his FL8-specific CTL used the TCR gene Vβ13.2 when sampled in July 2000 [Dong2004, PMID: 15596521].

Donor 007 of an Oxford cohort had an immunodominant response for several years, and recognized both the index and variant epitopes for KRWIIMGLNK. [Goulder1997e, PMID: 9416500]. This ART-naive 38-yo hemophiliac patient was infected in 1983 through an HIV-contaminated batch of Factor VIII.  After 12 years of being asymptomatic, with stable CTL response but slowly declining CD4 levels, he rapidly declined to 60 cells/µl with a high VL of 530,965 copies/ml.  All through he was able to elicit a response to KRWIIMGLNK, his immunodominant response, but response became attenuated at seroconversion. At progression to disease, Donor 007's hitherto unchanging autologous proviral sequence in the immunodominant epitope changed and fixed to 100%, with a K2M6 single mutation. The P2 mutation did affect B*27 binding. 007 is reported to express B*2705 [Goulder1997b, PMID: 9018241].

Donor 008 from an Oxford study was a hemophiliac infected by an HIV-contaminated batch of Factor VIII. Donor 008 made no response to a dominant HLA-A*0201 epitope and they carried a variant in proviral DNA. [Goulder1997e, PMID: 9416500]. This patient responded predominantly to Pol epitopes (2 Gag epitopes, 21 Pol epitopes).  [Goulder1997, PMID: 9126923]. Patient 008 carried the B8 MHC I allele which recognizes an epitope GPKVKQWPL. Upon mutation it is no longer recognized. [Meier1995, PMID: 7481824].

Patient 023 is one of a pair of HIV-1 positive, ART-naive, HLA-identical hemophiliac brothers infected in between August and December 1983 after infusion of a batch of HIV-contaminated Factor VIII. Age at time of infection was 10 yo. Identical twin brother, also a donor, is Patient 003. Study conducted at ages 25 through 27. This patient responded to Pol epitopes. (3 Gag epitopes, 10 Pol epitopes)  [Goulder1997, PMID: 9126923].

B*2705-expressing donor 025 carried 100% of the escape variant K2M6 form of the epitope B27-KkWIIMGLNK in studies in 1983, and had an increasingly VL by 1996, whereas earlier he responded to KRWIILGLNK, he lost this response as VL increased and CD4 counts became zero - loss of control correlated with the gain of the K2 mutation. Eventually after loss of asymptomatic status the patient died after two episodes of pneumocystis carinii pneumonia infection in November 1996. He was the hemophiliac brother of Donor 049, both infected by the HIV-contaminated batch of Factor VIII in 1983. [Goulder1997b, PMID: 9018241].
Patient 031 was infected for at least 13 years, and CD4+ T cell count was 410 cells/µl. [Goulder1996, PMID: 8959245].
Patient 032 was infected for at least 13 years, and CD4+ T cell count was 1190 cells/µl. with immunodominance to ISPRTLNAW. [Goulder1996, PMID: 8959245].

Donor 106 was an asymptomatic HIV+ homosexual male whose viral load (VL) was 21,000 RNA copies/mL in August 1996, with CD4 count being 110 x 10<sup>6</sup> prior to treatment. The patient mounted an immunodominant response versus novel epitope WASRELERF. [Goulder1997a, PMID: 9189221]. Donor 106 was asymptomatic and had been infected for approx. 6 yrs. Around Dec. 1996, he began treatment (zidovudine and didanosine) and his viral load became undetectable at <400 copies/mL. LANL database note - this patient is not linked with any antibody or T-cell epitope record i.e. they have not shown immunogenic response to any database-annotated, tested epitope.

Long-term non-progressor, HIV-1-infected volunteer Donor 200 first tested positive in January 1987 with a CD4 count of 552 cells/µl, VL of 928 cell/ml. [Papagno2002, PMID: 12452843]. With a CD4 count of 569, and a VL of 1,980, 96% of her FL8-specific CTL used the TCR gene Vβ13.2 when sampled in May 2002 [Dong2004, PMID: 15596521].
Patient 220 was infected for at least 10 years, and CD4+ T cell count was 480 cells/µl. [Goulder1996, PMID: 8959245].

Donor 84 is classified as elite neutralizer from whom the bnAbs PGT141–145 as well as the PGDM1400 series were isolated [Sok2014, PMID: 25422458]; they were infected for at least 3 years, at least 18 years old and asymptomatic without ARV at time of enrollment and when the antibodies were derived; infected with clade C [Personal communication, Dr. Elise Landais]. Escape viruses from Ab PGT145 were also found in this patient, referred to as Donor 584 in the following publication [Andrabi2015, PMID: 26588781]. Confirmed to be a Zambian male [Personal communication, Dr. Elise Landais].

Donor 868 was an asymptomatic seropositive homosexual male in whom 2 immunodominant responses were seen, one against VIPMFSAL.  CD4 count in May 1996 was 230 x 10<sup>6</sup> and viral load (VL) was 193,000 copies RNA/ml plasma.  ART commenced in 1996 with zidovudine plus didanosine.  In March 1997, VL was 5,000-5,500 and CD4 cell count was 400-500x10<sup>6</sup>/L. [Goulder1997f, PMID: 9412710].  Donor 868 responded predominantly to Gag epitopes (38 Gag epitopes, 23 Pol epitopes). A SLYNTVATL CTL-line was generated from Donor 868 too.  [Goulder1997, PMID: 9126923].  This patient carried mostly the index form B27-KRWIIMGLNK in studies in 1989, but had a high VL of 193,568 copies/ml with generalized lymphadenopathy since 1993. [Goulder1997b, PMID: 9018241].
Donor 90.17 is HIV-2+ and CTLs from her recognize HIV-2 and cross-recognize HIV-1 peptides from Gag p17 and p24.[RowlandJones1995, PMID: 7584954].
Patient 998 was infected for at least 7 years, and CD4+ T cell count was 400 cells/µl. [Goulder1996, PMID: 8959245].
HIV-positive for 6 years, asymptomatic and 31 years old at time of antibody derivation. Described by Burton1991 [PMID 1719545].

African donor enrolled approximately 4 weeks after infection and followed for over 6 years. During this time viral load ranged from 14,460 to 847,279 copies/ml (median = 173,667 copies/ml), and CD4 counts ranged from 69 to 431 cells/mm3 (median = 294 cells/mm3). A single founder virus is estimated to have established HIV-1 clade C with development of autologous neutralizing antibodies at 14 weeks; And CH103, CH104, CH105, CH106 isolated 136 weeks post-infection. Antibody CH235 was isolated from the patient's weak 41-peripheral blood memory B cells in culture. [Gao2014, PMID: 25065977]. CH103-class antibodies, in particular CH103, CH104, CH106 were previously isolated from this donor [Zhou2015, PMID: 26004070]. CH505 was among the 7/12 patients that developed high-breadth serum neutralization; infection year 2008; serum neutralization data and a list of sampled timepoints with viral load and CD4 counts was published by Wagh et al. [Wagh2018a, PMID: 30355496].
MAbs 98.6, 120.16, and 126.6 were obtained from a single individual (Eddleston1993).
Gorny2012 states that the following 3 mAbs were obtained from the same subtype B-infected participant: 1361, 1393A, 1357.

At time of leukapheresis for MAb 10E8, patient had been infected with HIV-1 for 20 years; infected with clade B; selected because his serum neutralizing activity was among the most potent and broad in the cohort.

Note: Among a cohort of former plasma donors infected between 1992-1995, the 3 subjects with the most broad and potent polyclonal neutralization were DRVI01, DRVI02, and DRVI03, all with CD4bs-focused plasma neutralizing activity [Hu2012 PMID: 22791603]. DRVI01 was selected from among the top 5% of serum neutralizers in a cohort of long term non-progressors; had been infected 10 years without treatment; entered the study at age 40 and was followed for 5 years (2005-2009); infected with clade B'. bnAb DRVIA7 was derived from a 12/2009 sample [Kong2016 PMID: 27067056]. A single leukapheresis sample was obtained 4.5 years after initial diagnosis with clade B HIV-1 infection, at age 44. IgG purified from donor EB179 was exceptional in its neutralization potency and breadth, ranking within the top 2% of the cohort. At the time the sample was collected, donor EB179 had 1038 CD4 T cells/mm3 and a viral load of 3180 copies/ml and was not receiving ARV; HLA typing revealed HLA A*02:01, A68:02; B*07:02, B53:01; C04:01, C07:02; DRB11:01, DRB15:01 [Freund2015 PMID: 26516768].  44-yo male participant 330183 had been diagnosed with an HIV-1 infection 11 yrs earlier and was not on ART. CD4 count, VL and HLA alleles matched Fruend2015 [Li2023, PMID: 37217466].

Freund2017 states: diagnosed with HIV-1 clade B in 1986; received treatment with didanosine and stavudine between 1995 and 1998, but has had no ARV since that time; IgG from donor was first tested for neutralization in 2006 when viral load was <400 copies/ml; neutralizing activity increased between 2006 and 2010, and has remained broad and potent since that time; also denoted as donor 622800 from the HIV Controller Consortium. [Freund2017, PMID: 28100831].

A 59 year old Zambian male presented with a two month history of fatigue and weight loss. During chemotherapy for myeloma, screening uncovered HIV-1 seropositivity with a viral load <50. The patient had been resident in the UK for 10 years during which time he had not experienced HIV-associated opportunistic infections. Author communication gave patient code as EC01. EN01 (alias 172T) was the donor with the highest mean serum neutralization from among a cohort of over 2000 people living with HIV [Schommers2023 PMID: 37957379]. They were age 21 and off ART at the time of sampling [Gieselmann2025 PMID: 41053396]. They were the top elite neutralizer from Mbeya region in southern Tanzania. Serum IgG from donor EN01 displayed the highest levels of breadth (100%) and potency (mean neutralization of 99% at 300 μg/ml) against the 12-strain global HIV-1 pseudovirus panel [Gieselmann2026 PMID: 41634487].

EN02 (alias 240F) was the donor with the second-highest mean serum neutralization from among a cohort of over 2000 people living with HIV [Schommers2023 PMID: 37957379]. They were age 21 and off ART at the time of sampling, and were infected with clade C. EN02 serum IgG displayed broad and potent activity with a coverage of 100% and a mean serum IgG neutralization of 94% against the 12-strain global HIV-1 pseudovirus panel [Gieselmann2025 PMID: 41053396].

Patient with broad serum neutralization, but normal progression; no  named cohort identified; infected with subtype B [Hutchinson2019 PMID 31156622]. Sequences from EN1 are shown in Fig. 1 in Hutchinson, and came from Mesa et al. 2019 [PMID: 30969970].

Male, born in 1973, started injecting drug use in 1997; diagnosed as HIV-positive in March 2001. Despite being diagnosed HIV-positive for over 20 years, CD4 counts remained at a high level, ranging from 692 to 934 cells/ul. The viral load of the patient only marginally increased from 14,700 copies/ml in 2016 to 35,000 copies/ml in 2020. The patient's viruses (CRF07_BC) were tested for neutralization resistance/sensitivity against bnAbs 2G12, PGT121, PGT135, 10E8, VRC01, and 12A21 [Wang2022 PMID: 35372102].
CRF01_AE-infected subject GX2016EU04 was ART-naive at time of PBMCs sampling and had serum neutralizing breadth >90% against diverse clades of HIV-1 pseudoviruses [Ju2018, PMID: 30382080].

Allard2012 discusses a study subject who had an immunogenic response to a Rev epitope, EELLKTVRL (Rev EL9). He was known as Patient ID H in Allard2008. This study subject was put on ART 8 weeks after diagnosis and at 5 years post-treatment he had a viral load of >50,000 copies/ml as well as a CD4+ T cell count that was at its lowest at 410 cells/ml.  He chose ATI after receiving a therapeutic vaccine that consisted of Tat/Rev/Nef electroporated autologous dendritic cells (DC) four times monthly.  Viral replication was not controlled up to 124 weeks post-ATI [Allard2012, PMID: 22309258]. Patient ID H recruited from the AIDS Unit of the Department of Internal Medicine (Universitair Ziekenhuis Vrije Universiteit Brussel, Brussels, Belgium) had undergone HAART for at least four years, comprising zidovudine+lamivudine+abacavir, and was 43 years of age when he consented to vaccine immunotherapy [Allard2008, PMID: 18539368].

Participant 18869 (H18869, Patient F ) was a source of longitudinal sequences pSC (post-seroconversion), with CXCR4-using HIV-1 variants developing in time. No cART (combination ART) was administered during this study. [Edo-Matas2010, PMID: 20638697].

This patient was denoted as C1 by Euler 2012, which also noted that 1986 was the year of seroconversion [Euler2012, PMID: 22156522]. A male sercoconverting patient who was ART-naive from whom samples were collected 11, 24,36, 49 months post-seroconversion, 18877 was the patient from whom a consensus sequence from env sequences of five clonal isolates obtained two months post-SC was generated, to produce the AMC009 SOSIP trimer [vanSchooten2022, PMID: 36395347]. Individual H18877 was infected with a clade B HIV-1 and enrolled in the Amsterdam Cohort Studies. Some cross-neutralizing activity was detected in H18877’s serum as early as 3 months post SC, and within the first year after infection the serum of this individual potently neutralized various viruses from different clades. Individual H18877 was categorized as an HIV-1 infected elite neutralizer as their serum at month 35 post SC neutralized a panel of viruses representing global HIV-1 diversity. Longitudinal env genes from this patient were sequenced and the consensus sequence of env genes from 5 viral clones isolated 2 months post SC was used to generate the AMC009 SOSIP trimer. PBMC samples of patient H18877 at 24 and 36 months post SC were available for B-cell sorting [vanSchooten2022a PMID: 35922441]. H18877 entered the ACS while HIV-1 negative but seroconverted during active follow-up. Individual H18877 was under observation for more than 5 years without receiving antiretroviral therapy (ART); in that period, his CD4  T-cell count was stable, and he had a detectable viral load. He had no protective HLA type and was homozygous for the CCR5 gene [Schorcht2020, PMID: 32999024].

Participant 18969 (H18969) was on combination ART (cART) soon after 20 months pSC, however no cART was administered during this study.  Changes in gp160 length and PNGS (potential N-linked glycosylation sites) were seen over time. Cross-reactive neutralizing activity developed as early as 12 months pSC. Patient D never developed CXCR4-using HIV-1 variants. [vanGils2010, PMID: 20071586]. Known as Patient D in a study of divergence of viral sequences [Edo-Matas2010, PMID: 20638697]. Seroconversion 1987; when sampled at 1.8 months post-seroconversion patient serum neutralized 5/6 heterologous viruses [van den Kerkhof2013 PMID: 24059682]. ##LANL database note: alias 1031 is from the LANL Sequence Database, and is the sequence name on the ACH18969 sequence accessions given by van den Kerkhof2013.##

Participant 19554 (H19554) carried both R5 and X4 HIV-1 variants at 5.5 and 7 years pSC (post-seroconversion). The patient was on combination ART (cART) soon after 20 months pSC, succumbing within 120 months (10 years) pSC, however no cART was administered during this study.  Changes in gp160 length and PNGS (potential N-linked glycosylation sites) were seen over time. [vanGils2010, PMID: 20071586]. Known as Patient B in a study of divergence of viral sequences and his serum was demonstrated to have cross-reactive neutralizing activity, detected 23 months pSC. [Edo-Matas2010, PMID: 20638697]. Higher sensitivity to serum neutralization was seen in one lineage of HIV-1 variants in this patient, coinciding with shorter Env V1/V2 and fewer PNGS. [vanGils2011a, PMID: 21653664].

Participant 19642 (H19642) carried both R5 and X4 HIV-1 variants just before clinical AIDS onset, but only R5 from earlier timepoints pSC (post-seroconversion). The patient was on combination ART (cART) soon after 20 months pSC succumbing around 160 months pSC but no cART was was administered during this study.  Changes in gp160 length and PNGS (potential N-linked glycosylation sites) were seen over time but plateaued. [vanGils2010, PMID: 20071586]. Known as Patient A in a study of divergence of viral sequences and his serum was demonstrated to have cross-reactive neutralizing activity, detected 23 months pSC. [Edo-Matas2010, PMID: 20638697].

Participant 19663 (H19663) was on combination ART soon after 20 months pSC (post-seroconversion). Changes in gp160 length and PNGS (potential N-linked glycosylation sites) were seen over time in viral variants not just due to changes in variable loops but also due to minor insertions in the C3 region. Cross-reactive neutralizing activity developed only 59 months pSC. [vanGils2010, PMID: 20071586].

Seroconversion 1986; when sampled at 10.5 months post-seroconversion patient serum neutralized 6/6 heterologous viruses [van den Kerkhof2013 PMID: 24059682].
